publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Selection; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Selection: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Separate if statements and one if-else-if chain always have identical behavior when several conditions are true.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Choose independent or mutually exclusive branches deliberately, order overlapping conditions from specific to broad, and test every boundary and default path.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Choose independent or mutually exclusive branches deliberately, order overlapping conditions from specific to broad, and test every boundary and default path.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Selection instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Choose independent or mutually exclusive branches deliberately, order overlapping conditions from specific to broad, and test every boundary and default path.
Separate if statements and one if-else-if chain always have identical behavior when several conditions are true.

answer.explanation: Choose independent or mutually exclusive branches deliberately, order overlapping conditions from specific to broad, and test every boundary and default path.
